researchBoundary: Proposal-only governance step. Taxonomy v5 remains byte-identical; the post-2005 scope is inactive and no observations, feature rows, candidates, scores or outer OOS are read or produced.

evidenceAssertions.summary: The contemporaneous Q2 2012 banking profile documents aggregate industry earnings, capital and asset-quality conditions independently of the JPMorgan event investigation and supports a system-level containment review.

evidenceAssertions.provider: Board of Governors of the Federal Reserve System
evidenceAssertions.summary: The dated H.8 release provides contemporaneous aggregate bank credit and deposit tables for testing whether the firm-specific loss coincided with banking-system contraction; no observation is opened in this proposal step.

evidenceAssertions.provider: United States Senate Permanent Subcommittee on Investigations
evidenceAssertions.summary: The investigation documents severe synthetic-credit losses, risk-control failures and supervisory shortcomings at JPMorgan, preventing firm-level calm from being confused with system-level containment.

evidenceAssertions.provider: Board of Governors of the Federal Reserve System
evidenceAssertions.summary: The supervisory assessment dates the counterparty default, places most reported bank losses outside the United States and separately describes stable, well-capitalized United States banking organizations.
The independently published Q2 2021 banking profile supplies contemporaneous aggregate capital, profitability and asset-quality evidence for a later reviewer; observations remain unopened here.

evidenceAssertions.provider: Board of Governors of the Federal Reserve System
evidenceAssertions.summary: The same assessment records a rapid counterparty default and more than ten billion dollars of losses across several banks, which must be weighed against the proposed United States system-containment label.

bankingHardNegativeBlueprints.boundaryRationale: The interval starts with public disclosure of the synthetic-credit loss and ends after the immediate loss escalation and management response. The label is limited to aggregate United States banking-credit containment and does not deny material firm-level losses or control failures.

The interval covers the March counterparty default through the immediate disclosure and supervisory assessment window. The proposed state concerns containment in the aggregate United States banking-credit mechanism, while preserving the documented losses and risk-management failures as counterevidence.
